title: Financial Leak Detection

content.statement: Most people lose money through invisible leaks.
content.question: What leaks would you find if you audited every recurring charge today?
content.equation: Leak Rate = Hidden Costs ÷ Total Spending
content.business_lens: Ad inefficiency, unused tools, bad hires.
sop: Pull your last 3 months of bank and card statements.
Flag every recurring charge and every ad or tool spend, no matter how small.
Calculate Hidden Costs as a percentage of your Total Spending (see calculation, page 5)
Cancel or renegotiate anything unused or underperforming this week.
Repeat this audit monthly, leaks reappear as new tools and subscriptions get added.
questions: What leaks would you find if you audited every recurring charge today?
Which subscription or tool have you paid for in the last 3 months without using once?
What's the smallest recurring charge on your statement that you couldn't explain if asked right now?
content.equation_example: Hidden Costs equals $400/month (unused subscriptions, wasted ad spend, forgotten tools), Total Spending equals $4,000/month. Leak Rate equals 10% out of 100%. Higher means a growing share of every dollar you spend is going nowhere. Lower means most of your spending is doing real work.

caption:
Principle Nº 151: Financial Leak Detection

Most people lose money through invisible leaks.

Ask yourself: What leaks would you find if you audited every recurring charge today?
